stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: First official ceasefire agreed after Zelenskyy push succeeds
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin paints Easter pause in fighting as Russia’s idea, but Ukraine’s president made repeated offers. What we know on day 1,507 Ukraine and Russia appeared to be on the brink of what could be their first official theatre-wide ceasefire since the February 2022 full-scale invasion after Vladimir Putin acquiesced to concerted offers by Volodymyr Zelenskyy of a pause in hostilities to mark Orthodox Easter, which is observed this weekend. The 32-hour ceasefire would start on Saturday afterno

stories.title: Orbán’s government accuses Facebook of undermining his reelection campaign
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s government is complaining his reelection campaign has been undermined by Facebook’s internal features favoring his rival Péter Magyar, who is ahead in the polls ahead of Sunday’s crunch election. The American tech giant’s “algorithm is basically working against the government parties,” Orbán’s Government Spokesperson Zoltán Kovács told POLITICO. Meta, Facebook’s parent company, denies the claims. The prime min

stories.title: What Trump’s blowup with Rutte was really about
stories.summary: President Donald Trump couldn’t get over European allies’ refusal to allow the U.S. access to military bases for the Iran war. And he made that clear to NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday in an hourslong, closed-door rant that focused on little else. Trump  made no specific demands , nor did he indicate the U.S. would reduce its commitments to the alliance due to base decisions, according to three people familiar with the meeting. But the president harped on the issue, according

stories.title: Wild animals and wilder friends: Viktor Orbán’s Brussels legacy
stories.summary: Welcome to Declassified, a weekly humor column . What’s the collective noun for Orbáns? An argument? A bother? A veto? Back in 2019-2020 there were two Orbáns around the European Council table: Hungary’s Viktor and Romania’s Ludovic (who, rather annoyingly in this context, does not have an acute accent on the “a” in his name). The latter is the brother of former European Commissioner Leonard (who was in charge of multilingualism, the have-a-seat-at-the-children&#821

stories.title: JD Vance’s claims about Orbán, the EU and Hungary fact-checked
stories.summary: US vice-president said bloc tried to ‘destroy’ country’s economy, despite it being a net recipient of EU funds During his visit to Budapest, where he heaped praise on the Hungarian prime minister, Viktor Orbán, days before the country’s decisive election, JD Vance claimed the EU was responsible for “one of the worst examples of election interference” he had ever seen. Standing alongside Orbán on Tuesday, the US vice-president said : “The bureaucrats in Brussels have tried to destroy the economy

stories.title: MEPs raise alarm about possible Russian meddling in Hungary elections
stories.summary: Von der Leyen urged to act over allegations of disinformation and intimidation on behalf of Orbán’s party The European Commission is being urged to investigate whether Hungary’s elections are being undermined by Russian manipulation, intimidation of journalists and voter coercion by the ruling party. Three days before decisive parliamentary elections that threaten the 16-year grip on power of the prime minister, Viktor Orbán, a group of MEPs have written to the European Commission president, Urs

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough on Ukraine, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: German Bundestag chief visits Baltic states to discuss cyber defence on NATO's eastern flank
stories.summary: Klöckner will also visit the German Bundeswehr brigade in Lithuania on Thursday. Around 5,000 German soldiers are to be deployed to Lithuania by 2027 to support the local military.

stories.title: How to watch the Hungarian election like a pro
stories.summary: Hungarians are heading to the polls on Sunday in an election that will potentially end Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. The race is being closely watched worldwide thanks to Orbán’s outsize influence as the preeminent EU ally of both U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin. In both Washington and Moscow, the Hungarian leader’s departure would be viewed as a setback. On the U.S. side, he has been directly endorsed by Trump and MAGA allies, who

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Poland's Catholic Church prepares parishes as crisis centres amid war fears
stories.summary: The Polish Bishops' Conference has established a special working group to develop an action plan in the event of an armed conflict reaching Poland, as "most Poles will first turn to the church for help, and only then to municipal institutions and offices," officials say.

stories.title: A kormánysajtó szerint egy ukrán szervezet zavargókat toboroz Magyarországra, de több ponton is sántít a sztori
stories.summary: Valóban volt egy fura álláshirdetés egy ukrán oldalon, de sok szempontból sem tűnik túl életszerűnek; egy orosz hamis zászlós művelet előkészítése logikusabb magyarázat rá.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor egy benzinkúton árulta el a Telexnek, mit szól ahhoz, hogy kiszivárgott a Putyinnal folytatott beszélgetése
stories.summary: Egyetlen szóval reagált a miniszterelnök a kérdésünkre, úton a debreceni kampányrendezvényére.

stories.title: Az ukrán Oscsadbank szerint meghamisították azt a videót, amit a NAV kirakott a pénzszállítókról
stories.summary: Közleményében azt írta az ukrán bank, hogy a felvételt feltehetően az egyik, Magyarországon feltartóztatott pénzszállító mobiltelefonjáról szerezték meg.

stories.title: Bellingcat: Magyar kormányzati jelszavak keringenek az interneten
stories.summary: Ez nem kormányzati rendszerekkel összefüggő szivárgás, hanem kormányzati felhasználók regisztráltak olyan oldalakra, ahonnan kiszivárogtak az adatok.

stories.title: Forgalmi változások Budapesten, Puzsérék koncertjének helyszínén
stories.summary: A Rendszerbontó Nagykoncert miatt egy sor menetrend szerinti járat más útvonalon közlekedik majd a Hősök tere és az Andrássy út környékén, de autóval is érdemes lesz elkerülni a helyszínt.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Debrecenben: Nekünk szükségünk van az erdélyi magyarok szavazataira is
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök lecserélte a mindenki hozzon magával még egy embert szlogent arra, hogy mindenki hozzon el mindenkit.

stories.title: Belülről ismerték a rendszert, aztán betelt a pohár – 16 év szembefordulói
stories.summary: Magyar Péter és Simicska Lajos történetére mindenki emlékszik, de nemcsak ők fordultak szembe a rendszerrel. Összeszedtük, még kik voltak azok, akik nyilvánosság elé álltak, amikor elegük lett a NER-ből.

stories.title: Szijjártó reagált a francia külügyminiszter árulózására, szerinte a magyar külpolitika pragmatikus
stories.summary: A magyar külügyminiszter azon is kiakadt, hogy Zelenszkij Kárpátaljára látogatott.

stories.title: A Pamkutya egyik tagja szerint az országunk évről évre egyre lejjebb csúszik, ezért kell elmenni szavazni
stories.summary: Osbáth Norbert a TikTokra feltöltött videóban azt is hozzátette, a mostani gyűlöletkeltés nem mehet tovább, és szeretne a jövőben egy békésebb, boldogabb Magyarországon élni.

stories.title: Vsquare: Több milliárdot költött a magyar állam egy Quvasz nevű hírszerző eszközre, ami végül óriási bukta lett
stories.summary: Az oknyomozó portál szerint a magyar titkosszolgálatok egy olyan szoftvert is használnak, amely a felhasználók tudta nélkül, a hirdetési adatok segítségével követi nyomon a felhasználókat.

stories.title: Orbánék próbálták kimaxolni Vance látogatását, de túl sokat várnak az amerikai kapcsolattól
stories.summary: Diplomáciai teljesítmény, ahogy az amerikai alelnök kiállt Orbán mellett a kampányban, és telefonon még Trumpot is bekapcsolta. Bíró-Nagy András szerint mégsem valószínű, hogy ettől a Fidesz új szavazókat remélhetne.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nehogy már Orbán Gáspár hagymázas gondolataira költsünk több tízmilliárd forintot
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke Győrben megemlítette a Telex birtokába jutott dokumentumot, amely szerint a csádi missziónak csak a logisztikája 21 milliárd forintba került volna.

stories.title: Political Capital: Biztosból bizonytalan választás lett a Fidesz
stories.summary: A Fidesz 2010 óta minden egyes parlamenti választás előtti kampányhajrában erősödött, most ennek épp az ellenkezőjét láthatjuk. Sokan várták az utolsó két hétben a kormánypárt csodafegyverét, ami majd a javukra billenti vissza a közhangulatot, ehhez képest a fejlemények egyike sem a kormányoldalnak kedvezett. A Political Capital kampányelemzése.

stories.title: „Mi lett azzal a furcsa menyasszonnyal?”
stories.summary: Közéleti versek a Telexen! Három magyar szerzőt kértünk fel arra, hogy a költészet napja alkalmából írjanak verset a hazai közéletre reagálva. Másodikként Tóth Krisztina alkotását közöljük.

stories.title: Germans pining for Gerhard Schröder forget his errors and ties to Putin. The SPD needs a fresh approach | Katja Hoyer
stories.summary: The former chancellor was the chief architect of Germany’s dependence on Russian fossil fuels and cuts to defence spending. Both haunt the country today The former German chancellor, Gerhard Schröder, is enjoying a curious political revival. Not so long ago, his reputation seemed in tatters. In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. Fellow Social Democrats (SPD) tried to expel h

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Nemes László következő filmje bekerült a cannes-i filmfesztivál versenyprogramjába
stories.summary: A 2015-ös Saul fia óta most először tér vissza a magyar rendező a legfontosabb nemzetközi filmfesztiválra.

stories.title: Első magyarként igazolt F1-csapat akadémiájához a 13 éves gokartos
stories.summary: Nagy Roland az Aston Martin akadémiájába nyert felvételt.

stories.title: „Duruzsol a miniszterelnök fülébe” – Lázár János száműzetésének és visszatérésének színfalak mögötti története
stories.summary: Lázár János 2018-as összeveszése Orbán Viktorral csúnyább volt, mint amennyire eddig tudni lehetett. Sok év munkába telt Lázárnak, mire visszajutott a hatalom középpontjába, és a Direkt36 kutatásai szerint ehhez Mészáros Lőrinc segítsége is kellett.

stories.title: 10 érdekes részlet a Lázár János visszatéréséről szóló Direkt36-cikkből
stories.summary: Mely fideszes politikusokat tartja puhánynak Lázár? Miként jött rá, hogy nem érdemes harcolnia Orbán Viktorral? Miben segítette őt Mészáros Lőrinc?

stories.title: Megfogta kézzel az Atlético védője a kapus kigurítását – a Barcelona edzője felrobbant a dühtől, mert nem kaptak tizenegyest
stories.summary: Hansi Flick szerint Marc Pubillt ki is kellett volna állítani emiatt. Kovács István azonban megkegyelmezett neki.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely Pálinkás Szilveszterről: Tehetségtelen volt katonának
stories.summary: A miniszter a Telexnek azt mondta: nem tárgyalt a kormány Csádról, nincsenek meg a feltételek a folytatáshoz, Orbán Gáspárnak pedig nincs szerepe a misszió előkészítésében, sem irodája a Karmelitában.

stories.title: A francia külügyminiszter szerint Magyarország elárulta az EU-t azzal, hogy Szijjártó segítséget ígért Lavrovnak
stories.summary: Jean-Noël Barrot azt mondta, egységesnek kell lenniük, ha erősek akarnak maradni egy olyan világban, ahol új birodalmak emelkednek fel.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerint sok pezsgőt fognak inni a választás éjszakáján
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök arról is beszélt az Indexnek, hogy szerinte bűncselekmény a kormánytagok telefonhívásainak kiszivárogtatása.

stories.title: Nyilvánosságra hozza a bicskei kegyelmi ügy aktáit a Tisza Párt, ha nyer a választáson
stories.summary: Magyar Péter korábban az országjárásán is beszélt arról, hogy felháborítónak tartja a bicskei otthon vezetőjének áprilisi szabadulását.

stories.title: Nem azt tudja az Amerikából vásárolt kőolaj, amire a Molnak igazán szüksége lenne
stories.summary: Az amerikai kormánynak tett gesztusként is értelmezhető a megállapodás, a szóban forgó mennyiség azonban nem túl nagy, valószínűleg a szállítási költség és az amerikai olaj összetétele miatt.

stories.title: Hungary betrayed EU by Szijjártó promising assistance to Lavrov, French Foreign Minister says
stories.summary: Jean-Noël Barrot said that if they want to remain strong in a world where new empires are rising, the EU member states must stand united.

stories.title: Wáberer György: Az nem létezik, hogy mindig 26-27 országgal szembe megyünk. Nem vesszük észre, hogy esetleg velünk van a baj?
stories.summary: Az egyik leggazdagabb magyar a Telex műsorában arról beszélt, hogy tiszta választást akar, ezért is tájékoztatja az embereket és buzdít a szavazásra. A Fidesz vezetését árulónak tartja, és szívesen fizet vagyonadót, ha a Tisza Párt nyerne.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
stories.summary: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: EU poised to slash up to €1.5B in funding to Serbia over democracy fears
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Serbia faces losing up to €1.5 billion in EU funding as the European Commission weighs pulling the plug over democratic backsliding and the country’s close ties with Russia. The Balkan nation isn’t a member of the EU, but having launched talks to join in 2014 it is eligible for funds and grants to help it push through legal reforms. A decision to suspend payouts would further complicate the EU’s so-called enlargement process, with countries like Ukraine and Montenegr

stories.title: British sentiment toward Trump’s US plunges amid Iran war
stories.summary: LONDON — A majority of the British public now believes the United States is a negative force in the world, according to new polling shared with POLITICO as the Iran war continues to strain the transatlantic relationship. Public First polling carried out for the London Defence Conference shows that more than half (53 percent) of Brits now view America as a negative force globally — up from 35 percent who felt that way in The POLITICO Poll in December. Fewer than a third (29 percent) of those

stories.title: Tories scramble to fend off Farage on their home turf
stories.summary: BASILDON, England — Britain’s Conservatives used to be able to count on the support of “Essex man.” But Nigel Farage has been spoiling that party. During a clutch of nationwide elections on May 7, voters in the southeastern England county will elect a new local authority as Farage’s right-populist Reform UK looks to end the quarter-century reign of the center-right Tories, and prove that they really can be the dominant right-wing force in future national elections. stories.title: Nato ‘very disappointing’, says Trump, in fresh attack ahead of Rutte speech – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Donald Trump has taken to his Truth Social platform again on Thursday to renew his criticism of the alliance . The US president posted that “none of these people” (which people is unclear), including “our own, very disappointing Nato, understood anything unless they have pressure placed upon them!!!”. Whether that relates to earlier reports ( 13.28 ) that Trump told the Nato secretary general, Mark Rutte, he wanted to see concrete commitments within days from Nato member

stories.title: UK navy foiled Russian submarines surveying undersea cables, defence minister says
stories.summary: John Healey says warship and aircraft forced Russia to abandon activity in North Sea in month-long operation A British warship and aircraft tracked and monitored Russian submarines trying to survey vital undersea infrastructure in the North Atlantic, ensuring they fled the area, the defence secretary, John Healey, has said. stories.title: OpenAI shelves Stargate UK in blow to Britain’s AI ambitions
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence company cites high energy costs and regulation for putting landmark project on hold OpenAI has put on hold plans for a landmark UK investment citing high energy costs and regulation, in a blow to the government which has put AI at the centre of its growth strategy. Stargate UK was a part of the UK-US AI deal announced last September , in which US companies appeared to commit £31bn to the UK’s tech sector, part of a larger series of investments intended to “mainline AI” in

stories.title: Netanyahu says there is no ceasefire in Lebanon as Israel launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: Israeli PM says he will continue to attack Hezbollah ‘with full force’ after attacks that killed more than 300 people Middle East crisis – live updates Benjamin Netanyahu has said there is “no ceasefire in Lebanon” and Israel would continue “to strike Hezbollah with full force” as the country’s military launched fresh strikes. stories.title: Israel's cabinet instructed 'to begin direct negotiations' with Lebanon, Netanyahu says
stories.summary: The statement from the prime minister's office comes on the same day that the Israeli military warned residents of Beirut's southern suburbs of imminent strikes and called for them to evacuate.

stories.title: Venezuela police use tear gas against protesters demanding better pay and pensions
stories.summary: The monthly minimum wage in Venezuela is 130 bolivars (€0.23), unchanged since 2022, and roughly 330 times lower than the UN poverty line of $3 (€2.56) a day.

stories.title: Spreading Islamist insurgency dominates Benin's presidential campaign
stories.summary: Cross-border raids from militant Islamists are increasingly threatening the West African state's stability.

stories.title: Montenegro wants to join EU for security, PM says
stories.summary: Montenegro’s leader says his nation wants to become an EU member less for traditional “fiscal” reasons and more for security. “Joining the EU … used to be a story about … fiscal support for your infrastructure,” Prime Minister Milojko Spajić told POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum in Barcelona. “But it’s actually not the case anymore.” Instead, Montenegro — which officially launched accession negotiations to the EU in 2012 and is the leading candidate for membership — is looking to join the bloc pa

stories.title: ‘Mental breakdown’: oil tanker workers stuck in Gulf for six weeks are reaching their limit
stories.summary: Seafarer tells of ‘impossible’ situation, with strait still so unsafe that crew would not cross even if told to sail Maritime and port workers: how is the Middle East conflict affecting you? ‘You can try to minimise the impact that this situation has on your mental health but it’s becoming impossible.” After six weeks stranded in the Gulf, one of the 20,000 seafarers trapped by Iran’s chokehold on the strait of Hormuz is reaching their limit. stories.title: Israel’s attacks on Lebanon should not be happening, says Keir Starmer
stories.summary: In article for Guardian, PM also calls for Iran conflict to become watershed moment for future UK security The Iran war is a warning: Britain must build resilience – at home and with our allies in Europe Israel’s continued attacks on Lebanon “shouldn’t be happening”, Keir Starmer has said on his visit to the Middle East, as he called for the Iran conflict to become a watershed moment for the future security of the UK. stories.summary: Reporters search for answers to why first lady chose to make speech – and whether president knew it was coming Melania Trump’s surprise statement denying she had any relationship with Jeffrey Epstein sparked confusion about why she had chosen to speak out, and whether Donald Trump knew that the first lady was planning to draw attention to a subject he has called for the public to move on from. stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran war ceasefire does not cover Lebanon, Netanyahu says; Hezbollah launches rockets at Israel

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria tighten relations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s visit to Syria was not only a diplomatic gesture but motivated by strategic considerations regarding Russia, once a strong supporter of the Middle Eastern state.

stories.title: US: Pentagon must restore journalists' access, judge says
stories.summary: A federal judge has said the US Defense Department has not complied with orders to allow journalists access to the Pentagon. New rules have prevented reporters from entering the building without an escort.

stories.title: UK says Russia ran submarine operation over cables and pipelines
stories.summary: Defence Secretary John Healey says there is "no evidence" of any damage to UK infrastructure in the Atlantic.

stories.title: Gaza mourns Al Jazeera reporter Mohammed Wishah killed iin Israeli drone strike
stories.summary: Gaza was mourning Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah on Thursday after he was killed in an Israeli drone strike that hit his car west of Gaza City a day earlier.

stories.title: Melania Trump denies association with Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: First lady Melania Trump denied that she had close ties with the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in a surprise public statement Thursday, calling suggestions to the contrary “lies.” “I have never been friends with Jeffrey Epstein,” she said to reporters gathered at the White House. She also denied any friendly relations with Epstein’s co-conspirator Ghislaine Maxwell. She said she had “casual” email correspondence with Maxwell and suggested any association was a result of their inhab

stories.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
stories.summary: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. stories.title: Woman with three deadly diseases has ‘remarkable’ recovery after cell therapy
stories.summary: Treatment reset wayward immune system of patient with life-threatening conditions, say scientists, in a world first A woman who lived with three life-threatening autoimmune diseases for more than a decade has returned to a near-normal life after a cell therapy reset her wayward immune system. The 47-year-old had had nine different treatments, none of which had a lasting impact, before receiving the therapy last year at University Hospital Erlangen in Germany. stories.title: Lidl to open 50 UK stores in year ahead – and its first pub
stories.summary: Almost 2,000 jobs will be created, with retailer vying to overtake Morrisons as Britain’s fifth largest supermarket Lidl is to open 50 new UK stores in the year ahead – as well as its first pub – as it aims to overtake Morrisons as the country’s fifth largest supermarket chain. The German-owned retailer has begun building a pub in east Belfast in response to strict local licensing laws that cap the number of premises that can sell alcohol. Continue reading...

stories.title: Man who groomed 14-year-old girl he met on Roblox jailed for 28 months
stories.summary: Police say case highlights online dangers to children after Carlo Tritta pleads guilty to making indecent images A man who obsessively groomed a 14-year-old girl he met through the online gaming platform Roblox has been jailed for 28 months. Carlo Tritta, now 19, kept indecent images of the girl and travelled hundreds of miles from his home in Eastleigh, Hampshire in order to turn up, uninvited, at her home in Manchester. Continue reading...

stories.title: US accused of pressuring Latin America to cut ties with Cuban doctors program
stories.summary: Cuba accuses US of ‘extorting’ countries in pushing them to axe deals with Havana to send doctors on medical missions Cuba’s foreign minister has accused the United States of “extorting” Latin American countries by putting pressure on them to cancel decades-old deals with Havana for the supply of doctors. Bruno Rodríguez said the United States was trying to “strangle” the economy of the communist island, which earns billions from its foreign medical missions, after several countries stopped depl

stories.title: Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups
stories.summary: New study describes what may be the first case of a unified community of chimps, in Uganda, turning on itself On a June day in 2015, primatologist Aaron Sandel was quietly observing a small cluster of the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale national park when he noticed something strange. As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance,

stories.title: X Factor star Chico Slimani found guilty of drink-driving by London court
stories.summary: Singer, 55, said he had broken his sobriety after three ‘devastating’ bereavements and has not drunk alcohol since Chico Slimani, a memorable X Factor contestant, was found guilty of drink-driving on Thursday, telling the court his arrest came hours after he broke his sobriety. The singer, 55, whose real name is Yousseph Slimani, reached the quarter-final of the TV talent show in 2005 and later released a No 1 single, It’s Chico Time. Continue reading...

stories.title: Vladimir Putin announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine
stories.summary: Kremlin proposes 32-hour ceasefire starting on Saturday afternoon – with Ukraine expected to agree to plan Vladimir Putin has declared a 32-hour ceasefire in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter weekend, after an earlier call from Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a pause. The president’s decree, released by the Kremlin on Thursday, orders Russian forces to observe a ceasefire starting on 4pm Saturday and lasting until the end of Sunday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Grace Tame’s foundation announces closure over funding difficulties
stories.summary: Closure comes weeks after former Australian of the Year and advocate for abuse survivors says she lost speaking engagements because of a ‘smear campaign’ against her Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Child sexual abuse survivor Grace Tame’s foundation has announced it is closing, citing challenges with long-term funding. The former Australian of the Year set up the foundation in 2021 – the year she carried the nati

stories.title: Smithsonian museum director to move to Guggenheim: ‘a moment of change’
stories.summary: Melissa Chiu, 54, director of Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden, led the institution for 12 years A museum director at the Smithsonian Institution in Washington has announced that she is leaving to take over at the Guggenheim Museum in New York. Melissa Chiu has been director of the Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden on the National Mall for 12 years. In an interview on Thursday, she insisted that her departure is not related to Donald Trump’s efforts to interfere with the Smithsonian. Co

stories.title: Jimmy Sullivan, Queensland state MP, dies at 44
stories.summary: Independent MP elected to the seat of Stafford in Brisbane’s northern suburbs in 2020 and disendorsed by Labor in 2025 Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Queensland state politician has been found dead at his home. Jimmy Sullivan, 44, was the independent MP for the Queensland electorate of Stafford in Brisbane’s north. Continue reading...

stories.title: Elon Musk’s xAI sues Colorado over new rules for artificial intelligence
stories.summary: Company claims law regulating AI systems, set to go into effect in June, infringes on its first amendment rights Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company, xAI, has filed a lawsuit against the state of Colorado over a new AI law set to take effect in June. The suit seeks to block the state from enforcing the law, which would impose new requirements on AI systems to protect state residents from “algorithmic discrimination” in sectors such as education, employment, healthcare, housing and financ

stories.title: Thousands of electric vehicles recalled in Australia due to battery fire risk
stories.summary: Hyundai issues local recall due to a fault with its battery management software that could cause a fire while the car is recharging or parked Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Thousands of Australian drivers will be asked to check their electric vehicle batteries as part of a global recall involving more than 100,000 cars. Hyundai issued a local recall for almost 5,000 vehicles on Friday due to a fault with its bat

stories.title: Artemis II crew hails ‘golden age of space travel’ as they prepare for return
stories.summary: Crew fielded questions of trip with ‘limitless potential’ from members of the Congress ahead of splashdown With the crew of Artemis II set to return to Earth on Friday following its historic 10-day lunar flyby mission, the astronauts celebrated their lunar expedition and hailed the “golden age of space travel”, saying they hoped the historic mission would inspire the next generation. Speaking from on board the Artemis II on Thursday evening, the crew fielded questions from members of Congress as

stories.title: Why the UK banned Kanye West - podcast
stories.summary: Lanre Bakare on the UK government’s decision to revoke Kanye West’s visa after Wireless festival booked him as a headliner When Kanye West was announced as the headliner for Wireless festival in London this summer, the backlash was immediate. “He’d been on a campaign of four or five years of antisemitic trolling,” Lanre Bakare , the Guardian’s arts and culture correspondent, tells Nosheen Iqbal . “Embracing neo-Nazi imagery, pushing out far-right conspiracy theories about Jewish people.” Continu

stories.title: Lava bursts forth as Hawaii’s Kilauea volcano erupts
stories.summary: Hawaii Volcanoes national park closed due to eruption of one of world’s most active volcanoes, located on Big Island Amber lava exploded over 200 meters into the air as Kilauea, one of the world’s most active volcanoes, located on Hawaii’s Big Island, erupted on Thursday. Lava fountains began to erupt from the volcano after 11 am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey (USGS). On Thursday evening, plumes of smoke and lava pouring downslope were observable on a livestream cam

stories.title: Hundreds search for wolf that escaped from zoo in South Korea
stories.summary: Local school closes in Daejeon city as hundreds of emergency service and military personnel scour area around O-World theme park where the wolf escaped from Authorities are hunting for a wolf after it escaped from a zoo in Daejeon, a South Korean city with a population of 1.5million. More than 300 people – including firefighters, police and military personnel – are taking part in the search operation, an official from the Daejeon fire headquarters said. Continue reading...

stories.title: A day in the life of a 19-year-old in ICE detention: ‘I feel that this nightmare is not going to end’
stories.summary: Olivia has been detained for months at the sprawling Dilley center in Texas. She has lost 20lb, and wakes up every day with a headache Each day in detention feels like 48 hours for Olivia. The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

stories.title: Argentina approves Milei’s glacier mining bill amid environmental protests
stories.summary: Legislative change backed by libertarian president makes it easier to extract metals in frozen parts of the Andes Argentina’s congress has approved a bill promoted by the libertarian president, Javier Milei, that authorises mining in ecologically sensitive areas of glaciers and permafrost, outraging environmentalists. The amendment to the “glacier law”, which was already approved by the senate in February, would make it easier to mine for metals such as copper, lithium and silver in frozen parts

stories.title: Son of British couple detained in Iran calls on Starmer to press for their release
stories.summary: Joe Bennett says ceasefire presents ‘very opportune moment’ to raise case of his parents, Lindsay and Craig Foreman Middle East crisis – live updates The son of a British couple detained in Tehran on espionage charges has called on Keir Starmer to prioritise their case in the “very opportune moment” of a ceasefire in the Iran conflict. Lindsay and Craig Foreman, from East Sussex, were arrested while on a five-day trip across Iran in January last year and have been held in Evin prison for 15 mont

stories.title: Russian GRU exploited vulnerable routers worldwide to steal sensitive information, probe reveals
stories.summary: An international investigation revealed how Russia’s GRU cyber unit, known as "Fancy Bear," managed to steal sensitive information from governments and the military using badly protected routers.

stories.title: Russia labels human rights group Memorial 'extremist'
stories.summary: Russia's Supreme Court deemed the storied rights organization Memorial "clearly anti-Russian" in a closed-door ruling. Memorial said the decision marked a new phase of repression in Russia.

stories.title: Iran war: Israel says it will hold talks with Lebanon 'soon'
stories.summary: Iran had accused Israel of violating the ceasefire by launching massive attacks on Lebanon on Wednesday. Meanwhile, Germany has said it is ready to re-engage with Tehran. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran claims victory but people fear what's next
stories.summary: As a tenuous truce takes hold after over a month of bombing, Iranians fear a wounded yet surviving regime will clamp down even more harshly on its opponents at home.

stories.title: International development aid suffers unprecedented 2025 dip, led by Trump gutting USAID
stories.summary: International development aid spending fell by more than one-fifth in real terms last year, the biggest dip on record. The US slashing its outlay by more than half set the tone, making Germany the largest overall donor.

stories.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Epstein in White House video
stories.summary: President Donald Trump's wife, Melania, denied having been friends with late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his associate Ghislaine Maxwell. The president told US media he did not know about the remarks ahead of time.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in Russia's war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: India news: Voting closes in Kerala, Assam and Puducherry
stories.summary: It's election season in India with voters in two states and a union territory headed to polls. Meanwhile, Dhaka has once again asked India to extradite former Bangladeshi Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. Follow DW

stories.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies after falling ill in Nepal
stories.summary: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.

stories.title: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.title: UAE urges full compliance from Iran following ceasefire announcement
stories.summary: The UAE is seeking clarifications on the US-Iran ceasefire deal regarding attacks across the Gulf, as well as on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as a “central priority”, as Tehran's blockade continues despite its commitment to reopen the strait to free navigation.

stories.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
stories.summary: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

stories.title: Trump again berates NATO, calls it 'disappointing'
stories.summary: Trump's outrage at NATO allies over their failure to join the Iran war had prompted fears he would seek to pull the United States out of the nearly eight-decade-old alliance.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: Germany has resumed diplomatic talks with Iran, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
stories.summary: Merz also said that the continued Israeli military campaign in Lebanon could jeopardise peace talks expected between the United States and Iran over the war in the Middle East.

stories.title: Cuba accuses US of 'extorting' Latin America by forcing countries to cancel doctors deals
stories.summary: According to official figures, some 24,000 Cuban doctors and other healthcare professionals were deployed in 56 countries in 2025.

stories.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
stories.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.

stories.title: US First Lady Melania Trump says 'I was not a participant' in Epstein sexual abuse
stories.summary: Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Trump's second presidency.

stories.title: Russia's president announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had previously signalled Kyiv's openness to a potential Easter truce.

stories.title: US has let in 4,499 refugees since October - all but three were South African
stories.summary: Trump has given priority to Afrikaners, a white minority group he says is being persecuted. South Africa has objected to his characterisation.

stories.title: Petrol and diesel prices rise again as concerns grow over ceasefire
stories.summary: Motoring groups have warned drivers not to expect a significant drop in costs soon.

stories.title: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
stories.summary: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

stories.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to truce for Orthodox Easter
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon on 11 April through Easter Sunday.

stories.title: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
stories.summary: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

stories.title: India proposes new rules to regulate news and political posts on social media
stories.summary: The changes will give government more power over news-related posts shared by podcasters and influencers.

stories.title: Hip-hop pioneer, Afrika Bambaataa, dies aged 67
stories.summary: The Bronx born rapper's global hit Planet Rock is credited with shaping hip-hop in the 1980s.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag still in custody
stories.summary: Court reporter Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with "insulting the president." His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

stories.title: Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time | Aditya Chakrabortty
stories.summary: Tribalism has not faded over the past decade. Instead, new research reveals our politics has become ever-more polarised and fractious On 23 June 2016 , the British voter changed. Before that day, they picked a party, usually red or blue. By that morning, only two tribes mattered: remain or leave. And they kept mattering long, long after the result was declared. Rather than bin those short-lived and now stale allegiances, voters made them their personas. No longer a “Labour man” or a “Conservativ

stories.title: Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China
stories.summary: Beijing’s powerbrokers are credited with winning Iran over, although one analyst says they were ‘pushing an open door’ Middle East crisis – live updates As the world struggles to make sense of what, if anything, was achieved by the ceasefire deal announced by the US and Iran on Tuesday, one major power that stands to win regardless is China. Beijing’s powerbrokers are being credited with pushing Iran towards agreeing to the ceasefire, bolstering its status as a regional mediator. In China’s tigh

stories.title: Gillian Anderson and Cara Delevingne to hit Cannes as auteur heavyweights dominate festival lineup
stories.summary: The 79th edition of the film festival will see work by Pedro Almodóvar, Hirokazu Kore-eda and László Nemes considered for the coveted Palme d’Or Gillian Anderson, Rami Malek, Cara Delevingne and John Travolta are expected to walk the red carpet at Cannes this year, as the world’s most influential film festival unveiled an auteur-heavy lineup for its 79th edition. Competing for the coveted Palme d’Or will be new films by heavyweights Pedro Almodóvar, Hirokazu Kore-eda, Paweł Pawlikowski, László N

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz not open, Abu Dhabi’s oil chief says as crude prices rise
stories.summary: Uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire pushes price of Brent crude towards $100 a barrel Business live – latest updates Middle East crisis – live updates The boss of Abu Dhabi’s state-owned oil company has said the strait of Hormuz is “not open” despite the US-Iran ceasefire agreed earlier this week, as uncertainty over the truce pushed the price of Brent crude towards $100 a barrel on Thursday. Sultan Al Jaber, the chief executive officer of the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (Adnoc), said passage

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
stories.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
stories.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

stories.title: Facts vs. influencers: Is the 'Dubai dream' really over?
stories.summary: Some residents in Dubai insist life is going on as normal in the eternally-sunny emirate during the Iran war. Others proclaim the "Dubai dream" over, never to be recovered. Who's right?

stories.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
stories.summary: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

stories.title: BTS kicks off massive 'Arirang' reunion world tour
stories.summary: K-Pop's biggest act is hitting the road with a new album and concert dates in 34 cities across the world. Over 40,000 fans packed into Goyang stadium near Seoul to watch the first show.

stories.title: Southeast Asia trusts the EU more, but Brussels still has work to do
stories.summary: Southeast Asia increasingly sees the European Union as a trusted hedge against US-China rivalry, but analysts say Brussels will need stronger political engagement to turn growing goodwill into real strategic influence.

stories.title: Fragile Iran truce brings brief relief to African markets
stories.summary: The truce between the US, Israel and Iran has eased pressure on African markets. But economists warn that deep‑rooted vulnerabilities mean the rebound may be short‑lived.

stories.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
stories.summary: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

stories.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
stories.summary: The four astronauts said they were returning to Earth with "so many more pictures, so many more stories".

stories.title: Ceasefire threatened as Iran closes strait again and Trump warns US troops are to remain
stories.summary: In a social media post, Trump said the surge of US warships and troops will remain around Iran “until such time as the real agreement reached is fully complied with.”

stories.title: Fiscal discipline as Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership
stories.summary: Fiscal discipline will be Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership, writes Danylo Hetmantsev, chair of parliament’s finance, tax and customs committee, in an op-ed for Euronews. He argues that Ukraine has already shown it can turn shadow economy reforms into real revenue.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Beirut kill at least 182 and wound hundreds
stories.summary: Lebanon spent the night digging through rubble after Israeli strikes hit busy commercial and residential districts in central Beirut late on Wednesday, killing at least 182 people and wounding 890, according to the health ministry.

stories.title: Mexico: Miner rescued alive after 14 days trapped underground
stories.summary: After 14 days trapped in a flooded gold and silver mine, a worker has been pulled out alive while two others have died.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.summary: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: Berlin opens Süd Ost Galerie, a new open-air street art landmark
stories.summary: Germany has unveiled a new open‑air street art destination in Berlin, where the 450‑metre Süd Ost Galerie now stretches along Grenzweg in the south‑east of the capital.

stories.title: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.title: Best-selling The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals true identity
stories.summary: One of the biggest mysteries in publishing is solved, as The Housemaid writer reveals her real name.

stories.title: Trapped miner rescued from flooded Mexican tunnel after 14 days
stories.summary: The 42-year-old gold miner was standing in waist-high water when rescuers found him in a flooded tunnel.

stories.title: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon leave more than 200 dead and 1,000 wounded
stories.summary: Lebanon is searching for survivors after Israeli strikes on Beirut, Tyre and the southern village of Abbassiyeh killed at least 203 people and wounded more than 1,000, the health ministry said, as the overall death toll from attacks since early March surpasses 1,500.

stories.title: UK and Norway foil Russian submarine plot to survey undersea cables in north Atlantic
stories.summary: Britain and Norway announced new joint naval patrols aimed at protecting undersea cables from Russia in December, with a fleet of at least 13 warships protecting critical infrastructure in the North Atlantic.

stories.title: Pro-Iran groups using AI to troll Trump and try to control war narrative, analysts say
stories.summary: Analysts say the memes appear to be coming from groups linked to the government in Tehran and are part of a strategy of leveraging its limited resources to inflict damage on the US.

stories.title: Confusion reigns over Hormuz shipping reopening as Iran issues new passage conditions
stories.summary: Normal shipping in the Strait of Hormuz has not yet resumed as per the ceasefire deal, as Iran consolidates its hardline, unilateral position on lifting its blockade in apparent contradiction of the rollercoaster ceasefire deal.

stories.title: Iran rules out any restrictions on its enrichment programme, nuclear chief says
stories.summary: The issue of uranium enrichment has been central to Western relations with Iran for more than two decades, with the US and its allies accusing Tehran of seeking atomic weapons.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Amazon to end support for older Kindles, prompting user outcry
stories.summary: The move means owners of Kindles released before 2013 will be unable to download new e-books.

stories.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Határsorompót állított fel a Széll Kálmán téren a Kutyapárt, hogy megmutassák, mi forog kockán egy újabb Fidesz-győzelemmel
stories.summary: A sajtótájékoztatón a párt nem jelentett be további visszalépéseket, világossá tették, hogy indulnak a választáson.

stories.title: ByeAlex: A vasárnapi szavazás egy sorsdöntő történés lesz hazánk életében
stories.summary: Az énekes hosszú bejegyzésben buzdítja követőit a választáson való részvételre, és arról is ír, hogy az apja arra tanította, hogy nyilvánítson véleményt a közéleti ügyekben.

stories.title: Pánikolnak az olajipari vállalatok, miután Irán hosszú távra bejelentkezett vámszedőnek
stories.summary: Irán kétmillió dolláros vámot tervez bevezetni a Hormuzi-szorosban, aminek lehetőségét Trumpék még nem cáfolták egyértelműen. A továbbra is lezárt szoros jövője így továbbra sem tisztázott.

stories.title: Lejárt a határidő: a 2022-ben győztes tizenkilenc ellenzéki képviselőből hatan maradtak talpon
stories.summary: A szombathelyi Czeglédy Csaba az utolsó pillanatban rántotta félre a kormányt, Hadházy Ákos, Csárdi Antal és Hiller István nem lépett vissza, így ők a Tisza jelöltjei ellenében is elindulnak a vasárnapi választáson.

stories.title: Egy trükk miatt lesz pubja a Lidlnek Észak-Írországban
stories.summary: Hatvan férőhelyesre tervezik, még idén nyáron megnyithat.

stories.title: Bedrogozva harapott meg egy rendőrt az autós üldözés után egy férfi, felfüggesztett börtönt kapott
stories.summary: Tavaly júliusban történt az eset Mátészalkán.

stories.title: Miért ne nézhetné meg egy fideszes a filmünket?
stories.summary: Az Itt érzem magam otthon két producerével beszélgettünk a film költségvetéséről, és arról, miért nem sejtették, hogy egy ilyen felfokozott közhangulatba fog megérkezni az alkotásuk.

stories.title: Putyin tűzszünetet hirdetett húsvétra
stories.summary: Az orosz elnök arra számít, Ukrajna is követi a példáját.

stories.title: A Volkswagen-csoport visszatér az olcsó villanyautók piacára
stories.summary: A Cupra Raval alig több mint négy méter hosszú, de hatótávja akár 450 kilométer is lehet két feltöltés közt, és a csomagtartója is elég méretes.

stories.title: Fontos részlet az átjelentkezők és külképviseleten szavazók számára
stories.summary: Olvasónk hívta fel a figyelmünket egy régóta létező szabályra, amit a Nemzeti Választási Iroda is megerősített.

stories.title: A Titanic katasztrófájához volt mérhető az 1910-es öröritói tűz, mégsem emlékszik rá már szinte senki
stories.summary: Több mint 300-an haltak meg, amikor egy húsvéti mulatság közben felrobbant egy petróleumlámpa, és lángra lobbant az egész csűr. A szervezők ki akarták zárni a bliccelőket és bámészkodókat, ezért az épület bejáratait egy kivételével eltorlaszolták és beszögelték.

stories.title: Hogyan óvjuk magunkat a csalódástól a vasárnapi választáson?
stories.summary: Minél kevésbé kedveljük a másik pártot a sajátunkhoz képest, annál elbizakodottabbak lehetünk a saját pártunk sikerét illetően.

stories.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

stories.title: Five teens to go on trial in Sweden over attempted murder of Iranian dissident
stories.summary: Sweden’s security service Sapo says Iran has ramped up its targeting of dissidents since the start of the Iran war, accusing them of conscripting organised crime groups to carry out “acts of violence” on Swedish soil.

stories.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles after declaring South 'most hostile enemy'
stories.summary: The back-to-back launches came after Pyongyang made it clear that it had no intention of improving ties with Seoul, whose government steadfastly expressed its hopes of restoring long-dormant dialogue.

stories.title: Tehran accelerates executions of political prisoners since start of Iran war
stories.summary: Iran executed 14 prisoners on political charges in three weeks since the war began on 28 February, including protesters and alleged opposition members amid a continued internet shutdown.

stories.title: Indonesia deports suspected 'mafia boss' Steve Lyons, wanted in Spain on drug charges
stories.summary: Bali police chief Daniel Adityajaya said Lyons was wanted in Spain as the alleged "leader of a large-scale transnational criminal organisation" engaged in drug trafficking and money laundering.

stories.title: Ceasefire offers Trump exit as Iran war becomes political liability
stories.summary: The ceasefire deal with Iran might come at the right moment for Donald Trump, as it could stop the erosion of domestic support for the President. But Trump is left facing scrutiny over why and for what did the US get into war.

stories.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
stories.summary: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

stories.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
stories.summary: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

stories.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
stories.summary: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. Th

stories.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
stories.summary: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported t

stories.title: Indian migrant workers hit by cooking gas shortages are leaving cities
stories.summary: Migrant workers say they are struggling to access cooking gas cylinders as the Iran war squeezes supplies.

stories.title: Explainer: What is in Iran’s 10-point ceasefire plan and will the US agree to it?
stories.summary: Two-week ceasefire comes after Trump spoke to Pakistan’s leaders, with China also believed to be exerting influence over Tehran Middle East crisis – live updates US and Iran agree to provisional ceasefire with Tehran saying it will reopen strait of Hormuz The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday barely an hour before Donald Trump’s deadline to obliterate Iran was set to expire, with Tehran agreeing to temporarily reopen the strait of Hormuz. Israel also agreed to the ceasefire,

stories.title: Europe cannot bet on a post-Trump US turning back to sanity | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. But it is a battle to the death. Tyranny will either break the spirit of the republic or be quelled by it. Since t

stories.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
stories.summary: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventua

stories.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: Alig bírnak a turistákkal egy japán városban, elhalasztanak egy cseresznyevirágos fesztivált
stories.summary: A turisták új életet hoznak az elnéptelenedő japán településekbe, de sok helyi arra panaszkodik, hogy a külföldiek szemetelnek és nyilvános helyeken vizelnek.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin feljelentést tett választási csalás gyanúja miatt
stories.summary: A dunaújvárosi körzet fideszes jelöltjét, Mészáros Lajost szavazatvásárlással vádolja.

stories.title: Sátoraljaújhely fideszes polgármestere felszólította Wáberert, hogy mondjon le a tisztségeiről
stories.summary: Szamosvölgyi Péter szerint tokaji posztjairól kellene lemondania az üzletembernek politikai megnyilvánulásai miatt.

stories.title: Orosz tengeralattjárók egy hónapon át ügyködtek brit tengeri vezetékeknél
stories.summary: A brit védelmi miniszter szerint a szabotázsakció sikertelen volt, folyamatosan figyelték a három tengeralattjárót.

stories.title: Krasznahorkait, Tarr Bélát és Bereményit ünnepli a Margó Irodalmi Fesztivál
stories.summary: Április 9. és 11. között több mint harminc, zömmel ingyenes programot, könyvbemutatókat, beszélgetéseket, dedikálásokat és koncerteket tartanak a Margitszigeten. Mi ezeket várjuk a legjobban.

stories.title: Reuters: Egy új kisebb, olcsóbb SUV-on dolgozik a Tesla
stories.summary: Olcsóbb lenne, mint a Model 3, de az akkumulátor is kisebb lenne.

stories.title: Loptak a Szőlő utcai javítóintézet kiürítése közben, fekete zsákból kerültek elő a holmik
stories.summary: Egy bv-felügyelőnél és két rabnál találtak dolgokat, a rendőrség kisebb értékre elkövetett lopás gyanújával nyomoz.

stories.title: Beazonosították a tizedik Facebook-oldalt, ami szabálytalan hirdetésekkel segíti Tuzson Bence kampányát
stories.summary: A Political Capital szisztematikusan derítette fel a minisztert népszerűsítő hálózatot.

stories.title: Visszalépett Czeglédy Csaba
stories.summary: A politikus függetlenként indult volna a szombathelyi választókerületben, de most azt mondta, nem tudna azzal a tudattal együtt élni, hogy fideszest segít győzelemre.

stories.title: Szinte valamennyi kormányablak nyitva lesz a választás hétvégéjén
stories.summary: Ha valaki nem találja az iratait, nem kell kétségbe esnie.

stories.title: Victor Vasarely születésnapján nyitott újra az op-art mesterének pécsi kiállítása
stories.summary: A jubileumi évadban a kiemelt pécsi rendezvények is reflektálnak a világhírű művész munkásságára.

stories.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
stories.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
stories.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
stories.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

stories.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
stories.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

stories.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
stories.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

stories.title: Rescued US airman treated in Germany after CIA located him in Iran by heartbeat
stories.summary: Using top-tier technology, the CIA was able to locate a US weapons systems officer in Iran after his fighter jet was downed. He is now being treated at the Landstuhl Regional Medical Centre (LRMC), the largest American military hospital outside the US.

stories.title: Thousands of Indigenous people march in Brasilia for land rights
stories.summary: Around 7,000 Indigenous people rallied in Brasilia against land grabs, mining and logging, pressing Lula to honour rights and protect the Amazon.

stories.title: Iranian attacks continue in the Gulf, raising questions about ceasefire
stories.summary: Euronews journalists in Dubai and Doha are reporting new attacks across the Gulf, rattling the short-lived relief about the US-Iran ceasefire announcement.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes rare visit to China in 'journey for peace'
stories.summary: Taiwan’s opposition leader makes historic six-day trip to Beijing to “boost dialogue and promote peace” ahead of a heavily anticipated summit between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Shipping companies 'see opportunities' but seek clarity on Strait of Hormuz reopening
stories.summary: Major shipping lines express cautious optimism over transit opportunities in the Strait of Hormuz after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced, but stress the urgent need for full clarity before any vessels move.

stories.title: Macron greets French pair back in Paris after 'terrible' three-year ordeal in Iran
stories.summary: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris, both teachers, were arrested in May 2022 at the end of a trip to Iran that their families say was for tourism and jailed on espionage charges.

stories.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
stories.summary: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

stories.title: Israel’s political forces are not happy with US-Iran ceasefire deal - here is why
stories.summary: In a rare moment of consensus, Israel’s political forces agree in their criticism of the US-Iran two-week ceasefire. And while the centre-left politicians are unhappy with PM Netanyahu, the conservatives are criticising Trump.
